summary: Reject `Predicate::DependencyExists` in admissibility so a hand-authored clause fails loud, not a silent `Indeterminate` no-op the spec fenced

edit.description: Treat `Predicate::DependencyExists` (~203) as a held-back predicate (like the full `pattern`): recognizable when parsed, but documented/flagged inadmissible rather than accepted as a working clause. Refresh the ~202 doc comment ('every declared dependency resolves') to name the held-back status.

In `inadmissibilities` (~108), emit an inadmissibility finding for a `DependencyExists` clause (a held predicate with no decidable reference syntax/extractor), so a hand-authored `dependency-exists` fails loudly. Today `decide` returns `Outcome::Indeterminate` for it (~333) — a silent no-op law 1 forbids. Update the inline test asserting `run(DependencyExists, ..)` is empty (~786) to the new fenced behavior.

tests.asserts: a contract clause using `dependency-exists` fails admissibility (a held predicate), never silently decides `Indeterminate`.

acceptance: A hand-authored `dependency-exists` clause is an admissibility violation (fenced like `pattern`), never a silent `Indeterminate` no-op; no embedded contract (rule.toml/skill.anthropic.toml) uses it, so self-host stays green; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Spec (10-contracts.md) fenced dependency-exists as held-back (no decidable reference syntax/extractor yet). engine.rs:333 returns Outcome::Indeterminate — the silent no-op law 1 forbids. Mirror the `pattern` fence. schema.rs emits nothing for it (no channel), so it stays as-is. Blocker FEATURES-COMPANIONS-DROP shipped (e65e872).

summary: Hoist the duplicate `sha256_hex` defs (skill/rule/import/drift) into one shared `&[u8]` helper — only the sha256 hoist, not the frontmatter round-trip helpers

new.description: A small shared `sha256_hex(bytes: &[u8]) -> String` util — the single home for the SHA-256 hex the import_hash/drift anchors compute (specs/20-surface.md law 5). Generic `&[u8]`, no kind-typing lost.

edit.description: Add `pub(crate) mod hash;` beside the other modules — this entry owns the `mod` line.

acceptance: One shared `sha256_hex`; skill/rule/import/drift all call it; no duplicate defs remain; hash output is byte-identical (no snapshot churn); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Do ONLY the sha256 hoist. The frontmatter round-trip helpers (split_frontmatter, pod_scalar_to_string, …) stay duplicated across skill.rs/rule.rs per the documented 'one artifact kind per module' tradeoff — accepted debt (.claude/rules/rust.md), not this entry. Blocker BODY-HASH-DROP shipped (e65e872); import.rs sha256_hex now at ~590.

summary: Drop the `role:` marker opt-in — the `MatchSelector::Role` variant + parse arms + roster read + empty-marker admissibility guard; `satisfies` is the sole artifact-side fill

edit.description: Drop the `MatchSelector::Role` variant (~463), its `selector_from` parse arm (~1538), the two test constructions (~2023/2468), and the doc reference (~190).

Drop the `matches()` `Role` arm (~463-464, reading `features.field("role")`), the empty-marker admissibility guard (~304-315, pure seam-policing), and the `MatchSelector::Role` doc mentions (~44/266/445). Adjust the `features()` test helper (~735) and the role-marker tests (~826/1447) to the name/glob path.
Remove/adjust the role-marker roster tests — fill is now `satisfies` opt-in or a `match` name/glob selector only.

tests.asserts: a requirement is filled only via a `satisfies` opt-in or a `match` name/glob selector; the removed `role:` marker path is gone and its empty-marker guard with it.

acceptance: No `MatchSelector::Role`; the role-marker admissibility guard is removed; a requirement's fill is `satisfies` (artifact-side) or `match` name/glob (contract-side) only, per the consolidated fill vocabulary; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Post-consolidation, satisfies is the sole artifact-side opt-in; match=name/glob is contract-side (10-contracts.md). MatchSelector::Role reads Features.field("role"), no struct field — clean delete over the unified Requirement. Blockers KIND-ENTITIES-RECONCILE (compose.rs) + FEATURES-COMPANIONS-DROP (roster.rs) both shipped (e65e872).

summary: Custom-kind units join coverage — read `[representation]` `satisfies` in `kind::Unit` instead of hardcoding it empty

gate.reason: follow-on; no story yet demands custom-kind coverage participation — deferred on priority

edit.description: Populate `Features.satisfies` for a custom-kind `Unit` from its projected `[representation]` rather than the hardcoded `satisfies: Vec::new()` (the commented gap ~451-454), so a custom kind (temper's own `spec` corpus) can opt into requirements — coverage is kind-blind by design. Confirm `import`'s custom-unit projection emits `[representation]` for the read to find.
A custom-kind unit declaring `satisfies` covers a requirement, exactly as a skill/rule does.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind unit's `satisfies` participates in coverage (kind-blind).

acceptance: A custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage exactly as a skill/rule does; temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: kind::Unit hardcodes `satisfies=Vec::new()` (kind.rs:451-454 commented gap). Coverage is kind-blind (spec), so the seam is legitimate — deferred only on priority. On revival rebase onto the unified Requirement; FEATURES-COMPANIONS-DROP (which edited kind.rs/coverage.rs) has shipped. Revive when the dogfood needs the spec corpus in coverage.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: npm entry with platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's distribution pattern); a small launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`).
Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infrastructure, mostly outside `src/` — the flume cargo gates (fmt/clippy/test) can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human-held creds (npm org, tap repo, signing). Parked until a human sets those up; the mechanical wrapper/launcher is buildable once they exist. `(binary-bootstrap)` is RESOLVED — this is execution, not a fork.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md`, typed frontmatter + byte-faithful body, with its own decidable contract

gate.reason: reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs` end-to-end. Split YAML frontmatter into typed fields (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`) with every unknown key preserved verbatim in `extra`, a byte-faithful markdown body, and `provenance { source_path, import_hash }`. Ship `from_source_file`, `to_meta_document`, and `from_surface_dir`. Body copied byte-for-byte; only the header rewritten. Identity is the path (file stem), not an internal field (`specs/10-contracts.md`).

The built-in decidable floor for the `agent` kind, embedded like `contracts/rule.toml`. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]` (harness fact); `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores (`globs`/`alwaysApply`); an advisory `max_lines`. No heuristic clause (`specs/00-intent.md` law 3).
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the `pub mod` line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents: Vec<Agent>` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*`, and add an `Agent(#[from] AgentError)` arm to `WorkspaceError`. Update the load tests.
Add `pub fn agent_features(&Agent) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features`: typed fields as scalars, every `extra` key folded into the name-keyed map, `body_lines` over the byte-faithful body.

Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to `<into>/agents/<name>/{meta.toml, AGENT.md}`, and add a `[[agent]]` lock row per agent — parallel to the rule path. Extend the idempotence snapshot to cover an agents fixture.

Wire the `agent` kind into `gate()`: embed `BUILTIN_AGENT_CONTRACT`, extract `agent_features` over `ws.agents`, compose the effective contract (floor ⊕ layer), extend the admissibility + conformance streams, add `"agent"` to `by_kind`, and add the agent floor to `schema`'s `floors`.

tests.asserts: Each decidable clause of contracts/agent.toml fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the embedded agent contract (conformance) and the agent contract passes admissibility; `temper import` scans, projects, and round-trips agents idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; `temper schema --kind agent` emits the agent contract; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction; value is meaningful contracts + the representation surface. Revive only if a story demands the `agent` kind — files/acceptance stay valid; mirrors the rule kind end-to-end. On revival re-check its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
